books.title: Drawing, Design, and Craft-Work
books.author: Frederick J. Glass
books.rationale: This is an excellent guide to art instruction, with suggestions for how to begin, materials to use, and the directions future work may take. This book is profusely illustrated with clear explanations.
books.noteText: The free link is a different edition, and the lesson plans will have different page numbers. Download the file to view the free version. It will likely appear blank in the PDF reader.

books.title: Brushwork: Elementary Brush-Forms
books.author: Marion Hudson
books.rationale: This book is perfect for those who are new to brush drawing. The exercises on gridded paper help students learn to mix colors and control the brush. Brushforms ("blobs") are used to create designs.
books.sharedText:
Art: Level 1
Art: Level 2
Art: Level 3
Art: Fast Track
Art: High School Fast Track

items.title: Picture Study
items.subject: Art
items.schedText:
1 time/week 10 min * 🅃 (Grades 1-3)
1 time/week 15 min * 🅃 (Grades 4-6)
1 time/week 20 min * 🅃 (Grades 7-8)

sections.title: Picture Study
sections.schedText:
1 time/week 10 min * 🅃 (Grades 1-3)
1 time/week 15 min * 🅃 (Grades 4-6)
1 time/week 20 min * 🅃 (Grades 7-8)
1 time/week 20 min *or_ 🅃 (Grades 9-12)

books.title: Redeeming Vision
books.author: Elissa Weichbrodt
books.rationale: Educators of all levels and high school students take the next step in deepening their relationship with art by contemplating a theology of art for viewers which impacts how we see as well as how we are and how we love.  Practice in taking what has been learned in previous years along with new aspects of art elements and themes is guided by Dr. Weichbrodt.

books.title: Art Print Set
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: This art print set contains high-resolution images that are in the public domain or used for Alveary with permission. The Art Print Set contains paintings by each term's artist: Edward Mitchell Bannister, Vincent van Gogh, and Lilias Trotter. We recommend printing on cardstock. Hard copies will be available with lesson plans.
books.noteText: Selections are also included in Our Work packets for Grades 1-12.

books.title: Our Work: Grades 1-2
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: This is a compilation of the year's Recitation, Picture Study, Hymns, Spirituals, and Folk Songs. Line drawings of artifacts from the historical time period are also included to provide students with inspiration for their Book of Centuries. We recommend printing or purchasing a copy for each student.
books.sharedText:
Recitation: Grades 1-2
Picture Study

books.title: A Passion for the Impossible: The Life of Lilias Trotter
books.author: Miriam Rockness
books.rationale: An inspiring biography of Lilias Trotter, a British artist who forsook fame to devote her life to Christian missionary work in Algeria, guided by faith, creativity, and love.

books.title: Van Gogh
books.author: Ingo Walther & Rainer Metzger
books.rationale: This comprehensive study of Vincent van Gogh (1853–1890) pairs a detailed monograph on his life and art with a complete catalogue of his 871 paintings.
books.noteText: Please note there is some minor nudity, and Van Gogh lived a tumultuous life, ultimately committing suicide.

books.title: Hurlbut's Story of the Bible
books.author: Jesse Lyman Hurlbut
books.rationale: Contains stories from both the Old and New Testaments in a very engaging narrative; omits sections that are unsuitable for younger children. The author adheres to the Bible without embellishment, opinion, or doctrine, making this volume appropriate for any denomination.
books.noteText: The free online option has different page numbers than the lesson plans, but the chapter numbers are the same.

books.title: The Bandit of Ashley Downs: George Muller
books.author: Dave & Neta Jackson
books.rationale: Through the fictionalized character of Curly Roddy, the reader is introduced to George Müller (1805-1898). Müller opened an orphanage in Bristol, England, with 30 children, but through faith and prayer never went into debt and helped over 10,000 orphans during his life.

books.title: Adoniram Judson: Danger on the Streets of Gold
books.author: Irene Howat
books.rationale: The first missionary ever sent from America, Adoniram Judson (1788-1850), faced many challenges as he ministered in the country of Burma. Although he faced many personal trials and even imprisonment by the government, he rejoiced that his translation work and ministry to individuals reached people with the gospel of Jesus Christ.
books.noteText: Please use Option 1 to utilize the discount code.

books.title: Reading Lessons: Level 1 (Revised 2025)
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: This program features 15-minute lessons focusing on phonics and high-frequency word recognition and utilizing classic stories and poems from the Free and Treadwell Readers. The lessons include spelling, copywork, dictation, and some grammar. This program reflects Mason's principles as well as recent research, especially the importance of phonological awareness and phonics.
books.noteText: Will also be available in Grade 1 Bundle with Alveary printing store after June 1st.

books.title: Reading-Literature: The Primer
books.author: Free and Treadwell
books.rationale: This is a great collection of folk tales and poems for a beginning reader. This book series lends itself well to Mason's approach to teaching children to read because of the repetitive pattern of the stories and the decodable text. Use with our Reading Lessons: Level 1.

books.title: Italics: Beautiful Handwriting for Children
books.author: Penny Gardner
books.rationale: We like this model because it is lovely, yet simple. Both the print and cursive models are in italic, making this a solid foundation for fine lettering later. Some may prefer a more traditional cursive model.
books.noteText: Choose this OR Getty-Dubay Handwriting Series.
books.maySubText: penmanship model of your choice or Getty-Dubay Handwriting Series
books.sharedText:
Penmanship & Copywork: Grade 1
Penmanship & Copywork: Grade 2
Penmanship & Copywork: Grade 3
Penmanship & Copywork: Grade 4
Penmanship & Copywork: Grade 5
Penmanship & Copywork: Grade 6

books.title: Getty-Dubay® Italic Handwriting Book B
books.author: Handwriting Success
books.rationale: For the beginning reader, this book presents lowercase, capitals and numerals. With a short word and lively sentence on each page, this book engages the student.
books.noteText: Choose this OR Italics by Penny Gardner. You do not need the Instruction Manual. Necessary instructions will be included in the lesson plans.
books.maySubText: penmanship model of your choice or Penny Gardner

books.title: Our Work: Grades 1-2
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: This is a compilation of the year's Recitation, Picture Study, Hymns, Spirituals, and Folk Songs. Line drawings of artifacts from the historical time period are also included to provide students with inspiration for their Book of Centuries. We recommend printing or purchasing a copy for each student.
books.sharedText:
Recitation: Grades 1-2
Picture Study
Music: Intro
Music: Level 1

books.title: What Makes Day and Night
books.author: Franklyn M. Branley
books.rationale: Through diagrams, pictures, and simple text, this book helps students gain a better understanding of how the rotation of the earth gives us day and night.
books.noteText: Teachers may wish to check this book out from the library rather than purchase it, as it is only used for a couple of lessons. Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

books.title: Elementary Geography
books.author: Charlotte Mason
books.rationale: This is Charlotte Mason's classic introduction to Geography. We use selections from the book, which include many demonstrations, to help young children understand the concepts.

books.title: Me on the Map
books.author: Joan Sweeney
books.rationale: This book explains the many ways in which we can describe where we are located. Beginning in a little girl's room, we gradually zoom out to her house, her street, her town, her state, her country, and her planet.

books.title: United States Atlas 7th Ed.
books.author: National Geographic Kids
books.rationale: This atlas is used across the curriculum as a reference and as a text to read in some lessons. With interesting information and pictures, this is an economical reference for students.
books.noteText: Optional for Grade 1. For U.S. students.
books.maySubText: earlier edition. Other atlases may be used; however, map work and other lessons refer specifically to this one.
books.sharedText:
Geography: Grade 1
Geography: Grade 7 (U.S.)
Resources: Grade 9
Resources: Grade 10
Resources: Grade 11
Resources: Grade 12
Geography: Grade 2 (U.S.)
Geography: Grade 3 (U.S.)
Geography: Grade 8 (U.S.)
History: Grades 2-3 (U.S.)

books.title: The Reasons for Seasons
books.author: Gail Gibbons
books.rationale: This book is a great introduction to seasons, the earth's orbit, and axial tilt.
books.noteText: Teachers may wish to check this book out from the library rather than purchase it, as it is only used for a couple of lessons. Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

books.title: Down Comes the Rain
books.author: Franklyn M. Branley
books.rationale: This is a great book for students to learn about the water cycle. There are clear explanations and great illustrations.
books.noteText: Teachers may wish to check this book out from the library rather than purchase it, as it is only used for a couple of lessons. Available on Learning Ally with Audio.
books.maySubText: Water Dance by Thomas Locker if you own it

books.title: Tales the Totems Tell
books.author: Hugh Weatherby
books.rationale: This excerpt contains the story of the first totem pole and the most common totem figure, the raven.
books.noteText: PDF provided for member use only under Fair Use Copyright Law.

books.title: Frozen Land
books.author: Jan Reynolds
books.rationale: Kenalogak, a young girl, is part of the Inuit culture in Canada. How do people survive in such a cold climate? Watch as Kenalogak's family finds food and makes shelter and clothing for their survival.
books.noteText: Used briefly. You may wish to check out from the library.

books.title: The Very First Americans
books.author: Cara Ashrose & Bryna Waldman
books.rationale: This book gives a brief overview of how humans came to North America and how the very diverse people groups lived.
books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.
books.scopeText:
Term 1 (Partial Term)
Term 2 (Partial Term)
Term 3 (Partial Term)

books.title: The Book of Indians
books.author: Holling C. Holling
books.rationale: Little Turtle lives on the cliffs of the desert. The other children make fun of him because he is slow and clumsy, so he plays by himself. One day, strangers approach Little Turtle's village. What Little Turtle does in response makes him a hero.
books.noteText: PDF provided for member use only under Fair Use Copyright Law.

books.title: Moonstick: The Seasons of the Sioux
books.author: Eve Bunting
books.rationale: A young boy comes of age under the thirteen moons of the Sioux year. With each notch in his father's moon-counting stick, the boy marvels at the world around him, observing the sometimes subtle, sometimes remarkable, changes in the seasons and in his own tribe's way of living.
books.noteText: Used briefly. You may wish to check out from the library.

books.title: Itse Selu: Cherokee Harvest Festival
books.author: Daniel Pennington
books.rationale: A captivating glimpse of Cherokee life in pre-Columbian North America. Join Little Wolf's family as they prepare to celebrate Itse Selu, the harvest festival. As night approaches the village, the magic of anticipation fills the air. The luscious feast, Grandfather's storytelling, and the sacred corn dance weave a magical tapestry of tradition.
books.noteText: Used briefly. You may wish to check out from the library.

books.title: Hiawatha and the Peacemaker
books.author: Robbie Robertson
books.rationale: This is the traditional story of how Hiawatha brought rival tribes together to defeat the evil Tadodaho and form the powerful Iroquois Nation in the 14th century.
books.noteText: Some images may be scary for sensitive children. Used briefly. You may wish to check out from the library. Available on Learning Ally with Audio.
books.sharedText:
History: Grade 1 (U.S.)
History: Grade 1 (Canada)

books.title: The Honour Drum
books.author: Cheryl Bear and Tim Huff
books.rationale: Written as a partnership between two friends--an Indigenous woman and a non-Indigenous man--this story introduces children to the history, stories, traditions, and symbols that have been meaningful to Indigenous people from the distant past right up to today. Its aim is to foster understanding, empathy, and appreciation, and to spark meaningful conversations with your children.

books.title: Thirteen Moons on Turtle's Back: A Native American Year of Moons
books.author: Joseph Bruchac & Jonathan London
books.rationale: In Native American legend, the thirteen scales on a turtle's back symbolize the annual thirteen cycles of the moon and the seasonal changes each one brings. The thirteen poems in this book -- one for each moon of the year, and each from a different indigenous group -- celebrate the natural world and weave together traditional knowledge and folklore. Each poem is illustrated by a beautiful painting.
books.scopeText:
Term 1 (Partial Term)
Term 2 (Partial Term)
Term 3 (Partial Term)

books.title: The Pencil
books.author: Susan Avingaq
books.rationale: In The Pencil, the Inuk filmmaker and author Susan Avingaq recalls childhood memories from her early life in the Arctic, living in an igloo with her family. Through this joyful story, Avingaq shows the importance of good stewardship, using what we have wisely, and the delight that can be found in simple objects.

books.title: Maple Moon
books.author: Connie Brummel Crook
books.rationale: In Maple Moon, Connie Brummel Crook borrows from traditional folk tales to create an enchanting fictional account of how maple syrup might have been discovered. The protagonist, a young Mississauga boy named Rides the Wind, learns of the sweet water because his physical limitations prevent him from joining a hunt; yet, it is this discovery that helps his community survive the winter. Illustrated by beautiful paintings, this book teaches children that strength can come in many forms.

books.title: The Gift of the Great Buffalo
books.author: Carole Lindstrom
books.rationale: Through the story of Rose, a Métis-Ojibwe girl on the prairie in the 1860s, The Gift of the Buffalo introduces young readers to Métis culture and the vital relationship between Indigenous communities and the buffalo, which provided sustenance and shelter.

books.title: On the Trapline
books.author: David A. Robertson
books.rationale: This is an award-winning picture book, which tells the gentle story of a boy accompanying his grandfather on a trip back to his childhood home where he and his family lived off the land.

books.title: Hiawatha and the Peacemaker
books.author: Robbie Robertson
books.rationale: This is the traditional story of how Hiawatha brought rival tribes together to defeat the evil Tadodaho and form the powerful Iroquois Nation in the 14th century.
books.noteText: Some images may be scary for sensitive children. Used briefly. You may wish to check out from the library. Available on Learning Ally with Audio.
books.sharedText:
History: Grade 1 (U.S.)
History: Grade 1 (Canada)

books.title: Paper Folding Video Lessons
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: Teaches basic paper folding techniques and helps young students gain practice in fine motor skills.

books.title: Sewing School
books.author: Andria Lisle and Amie Petronis Plumley
books.rationale: This book teaches basic stitches and techniques and includes lots of simple sewing projects for the beginner.

books.title: Tales from Grimm
books.author: Wanda Gag
books.rationale: Mason had her youngest students read fairy tales from Grimm or Anderson. This retelling by Wanda Gag is gentler and less scary than many other translations, while still having good literary quality and simple English for students to practice reading themselves.

books.title: Around the World in 80 Tales
books.author: Saviour Pirotta
books.rationale: This book takes readers across six continents with short, entertaining folktales to stir our first graders' imaginations with tales from many lands.

books.title: Aesop's Favorite Fables
books.author: Aesop, illus. by Milo Winter
books.rationale: Aesop's Fables have made learning life's lessons enjoyable for 2500 years. The illustrations by Milo Winter set this collection apart.
books.maySubText: another collection of Aesop's Fables. Lesson plans include the story titles.

books.title: American Tall Tales
books.author: Mary Pope Osborne
books.rationale: These larger-than-life stories capture something of America's personality. Mary Pope Osborne has retold these stories well, and the wood-carved illustrations are delightful.
books.noteText: U.S. Student Option. Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

books.title: The Spirit of Canada: Canada's Story in Legends, Fiction, Poems, and Songs
books.author: Barbara Hehner
books.rationale: Organized thematically, this beautiful anthology encompasses a wide variety of voices and stories that reflect Canada's past and present. It is an excellent accompaniment to Canadian history and literature, as it contains many different genres: poems, songs, letters, legends, stories, folk tales, historical accounts, and more.
books.noteText: Canadian students should purchase this book and NOT American Tall Tales for the Tall Tales/Fables course.
books.sharedText:
Canadian History: Grades 2-4
Tall Tales/ Fables

books.title: The Crescent Moon
books.author: Rabindranath Tagore
books.rationale: This delightful collection of children's poetry was translated from Bengali into English by Tagore himself. Lesson plans will draw from this collection and include links to additional poetry by this famous 19th century author.
books.sharedText:
Poetry: Grades 1-4
Poetry: Grades 5-6
Poetry Reading: Grade 7
Poetry Reading: Grade 8
Poetry Reading: Grades 9-12

books.title: Over the River and Through the Wood: An Anthology of Nineteenth-Century American Children's Poetry
books.author: Karen L. Kilcup (Editor) & Angela Sorby (Editor)
books.rationale: A beautiful collection of 19th century children's poetry for all ages.
books.noteText: Grades 5-6 can substitute A Poem for Every Day of the Year edited by Allie Esiri if desired.
books.maySubText: Public domain links
books.sharedText:
Poetry: Grades 1-4
Poetry: Grades 5-6
Poetry Reading: Grade 7
Poetry Reading: Grade 8

books.title: Gladiola Garden
books.author: Effie Lee Newsome
books.rationale: This book by a lesser-known American poet contains beautiful nature poetry and illustrations for children.

books.title: Emma's Poem: The Voice of the Statue of Liberty
books.author: Linda Glaser
books.rationale: This delightful picture book tells the fascinating story of the poem engraved on the Statue of Liberty.

books.title: RightStart Mathematics Level A 2nd Ed. Book Bundle
books.rationale:
RightStart is based on discovery using a wide range of manipulatives. It is an up-to-date, spiraling program that uses methods that are heavily supported by current research. 
➜  Students also need the RS2 Math Set.
books.noteText:
The "Video" link will take you to a YouTube playlist where a master teacher will show you how to teach these lessons. 
eWorkbooks available for multiple students.

books.title: Math Card Games Kit
books.author: RightStart
books.rationale:
Kit is recommended for those who do not use RightStart Math; kit is included in the RS2 Math Set. 
➜  Additional recommended game titles will be listed with the Lesson Plans for each level of mathematics.
books.maySubText: any games that allow students to practice math concepts and skills.

books.title: Aesop's Fables
books.author: Aesop
books.rationale: Contains illustrations to accompany the French literature story lines. The fables and illustrations included in the book will be used multiple years in French.

books.title: Linnea in Monet's Garden
books.author: Christina Björk
books.rationale: This beautifully illustrated children’s book introduces readers to art, history, travel, and the life of the famous Impressionist painter Claude Monet through the eyes of a curious young girl. The watercolor paintings and reproductions of Monet’s work provide a great way for students to learn French vocabulary during the picture study lessons.

books.title: French: Student Notebook
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: This notebook contains markable copies of lesson readings, vocabulary lists, and lyrics for songs.
books.noteText: This notebook is also available as part of hard-copy lessons in the print store.

books.title: Diez Deditos (Audio mp3)
books.author: Jose-Luis Orozco
books.rationale: This is a collection of traditional songs in Spanish.
books.noteText: Option 1 is a link to stream with Spotify, or Option 2 is a link to purchase the CD.
books.maySubText: favorite streaming service

books.title: Diez Deditos and Other Play Rhymes and Action Songs from Latin America
books.author: José-Luis Orozco
books.rationale: This is a collection of finger rhymes and action songs in Spanish.

books.title: Family Pictures/Cuadros de familia
books.author: Carmen Lomas Garza
books.rationale: The vibrant paintings and warm personal stories depict memories of the author growing up in a traditional Mexican-American family. This book is a great way to learn Spanish vocabulary during the picture study lessons.

books.title: Spanish: Student Notebook
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: This notebook contains markable copies of lesson readings, vocabulary lists, and answers to reading questions and grammar exercises.
books.noteText: This notebook is available as a hard copy with Spanish lessons in the Alveary print store.

books.title: Hymns, Spirituals, & Folk Songs PDF
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: Selections are also included in Our Work packets for Grades 1-12. Audio lessons and other resources are available in lesson plans.

books.title: Our Hymns, Our Heritage
books.author: David & Barbara Leeman
books.rationale: This student hymnal contains multi-denominational hymns organized by the church year, with brief histories of the music and lyrics, bios of the writers, related Scripture, and other ideas to meditate upon.

books.title: Like a Bird: The Art of the American Slave Song
books.author: Cynthia Grady
books.rationale: This is a collection of African American spirituals, along with their stories and beautiful illustrations.
books.noteText: 1-2 songs from this book are chosen most years.

books.title: The Book of Playground Songs & Rhymes
books.author: John Feierabend
books.rationale: This book contains traditional children's songs and rhymes used to teach rhythm, jump rope, and hand-eye coordination.
books.noteText: Song recordings will be available June 1.
books.maySubText: earlier edition
books.sharedText:
Jump Rope
Playground Songs & Rhymes

books.title: The Book of Playground Songs & Rhymes
books.author: John Feierabend
books.rationale: This book contains traditional children's songs and rhymes used to teach rhythm, jump rope, and hand-eye coordination.
books.noteText: Song recordings will be available June 1.
books.maySubText: earlier edition
books.sharedText:
Jump Rope
Playground Songs & Rhymes

books.title: The Book of Song Dances
books.author: John Feierabend
books.rationale: This book uses traditional songs, rhymes, and dances to teach children basic dance steps.
books.noteText: Instructional videos will be available June 1.
books.maySubText: earlier edition, but some dances have different page numbers.

books.title: Over and Under the Pond
books.author: Kate Messner
books.rationale: This beautiful picture book takes us on a trip to the pond alongside mother and son. It is a lovely addition to your bookshelf, but scheduling in the lesson plans is flexible for those using their library.
books.maySubText: any life-giving picture book about pond habitats as preferred by the teacher.

books.title: Bat Loves the Night
books.author: Nicola Davies
books.rationale: This beautiful picture book illuminates the life of a common but less-often-observed creature. It is a lovely addition to your bookshelf, but scheduling in the lesson plans is flexible for those using their library.
books.maySubText: any life-giving picture book about bats as preferred by the teacher.

books.title: Winter Sleep: A Hibernation Story
books.author: Sean Taylor
books.rationale: This beautiful picture book takes readers on a winter walk with a boy and his grandmother. It is a lovely addition to your bookshelf, but scheduling in the lesson plans is flexible for those using their library.
books.maySubText: any life-giving picture book about a winter habitat preferred by the teacher.

books.title: A Fruit is a Suitcase for Seeds
books.author: Jean Richards
books.rationale: This beautiful picture book introduces students to the ways that plants use seeds. It is a lovely addition to your bookshelf, but scheduling in the lesson plans is flexible for those using their library.
books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with VoiceText.
books.maySubText: any life-giving picture book about seeds as preferred by the teacher.

books.title: Up in the Garden and Down in the Dirt
books.author: Kate Messner
books.rationale: This beautiful picture book takes us on a trip to the garden alongside a girl and her grandmother. It is a lovely addition to your bookshelf, but scheduling in the lesson plans is flexible for those using their library.
books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with VoiceText.
books.maySubText: any life-giving picture book about garden habitats as preferred by the teacher.

books.title: A House Spider's Life
books.author: John Himmelman
books.rationale: This beautiful picture book illuminates the life of a common, but often less appreciated, creature. It is a lovely addition to your bookshelf, but scheduling in the lesson plans is flexible for those using their library.
books.maySubText: any life-giving picture book about spiders as preferred by the teacher.

books.title: Walks with a Naturalist: Living Nature Reader Volume 1
books.author: Melissa Dowland
books.rationale: Our nature reader brings learners alongside an experienced nature educator as she relates her stories with curiosity, delight, and humility.  Volume 1 explores insects, spiders, birds, seeds, and more.
books.noteText: The print book is formatted for easy reading; the eBook is formatted with enhanced typesetting (i.e. the print automatically adjusts to the device used to read it and the reader can adjust fonts, colors, etc).
books.maySubText: another nature reader that provides accurate, living ideas across a variety of creatures; supplemental links and prompts may not apply to substituted reader

books.title: US Wall Map (US students only)
books.author: National Geographic
books.rationale: This map clearly shows landforms, and its projection is more accurate than some other maps.
books.noteText: Hang map at student eye level.
books.maySubText: any US wall map that shows both geographical features and place names. Avoid maps with roadways, as these obscure other features.
books.sharedText:
Resources: Grades 1-3
Resources: Grades 4-6
Resources: Grades 7-8

books.title: Canadian Wall Map (CA students only)
books.author: National Geographic
books.rationale: This map clearly shows landforms, and its projection is more accurate than some other maps.
books.noteText: Hang map at student eye level.
books.maySubText: any Canadian wall map that shows both geographical features and place names. Avoid maps with roadways, as these obscure other features.
books.sharedText:
Resources: Grades 1-3
Resources: Grades 4-6
Resources: Grades 7-8

books.title: World Wall Map
books.author: National Geographic
books.rationale: The projection of this wall map distorts the image less than others. It also makes geographical features easy to find and place names easy to read.
books.noteText: Hang map at student eye level.

books.title: Salt, Fat, Acid, Heat: Mastering the Elements of Good Cooking
books.author: Samin Nosrat
books.rationale: Whether for afternoon occupations or High School Food Science, young cooks will be inspired by this well-written text with its engaging narrative and beautiful illustrations.  The author does a good job of introducing the basic ingredients of cooking without requiring a full chemistry course; students who have already taken chemistry will find additional connections to enhance their understanding even more.
books.noteText: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Audible.
